topic: Gemini 3.1 Pro Review 2026: Developer Benchmark and Coding Performance

competitors.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Review 2026: #1 Ranked AI Model?
key_points: Gemini 3.1 Pro launched February 19, 2026, immediately claimed #1 spot on Artificial Analysis' Intelligence Index across 115 models
Scored 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 — more than double Gemini 3 Pro's 35-38%, representing >100% improvement in core reasoning in 3 months
Major trade-off: 29-second time-to-first-token (24x slower than peer median) due to extended thinking architecture
Same API pricing as 3.0 Pro ($2.00/1M input, $12.00/1M output) — better performance at same cost
1M token context window for long-document workflows (equivalent to ~1,500 A4 pages)
Best suited for researchers, analysts, enterprise Google Cloud users, developers building reasoning-heavy agentic systems
Rated 8/10 overall with high reasoning quality (9.5/10) but poor speed/latency (5/10)

competitors.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro: Benchmarks, Cost, and Production Fit
key_points: Gemini 3.1 Pro more than doubled ARC-AGI-2 score from 31.1% to 77.1%, testing novel pattern recognition rather than memorized knowledge
Three-tier thinking system (Low/Medium/High) lets developers balance speed against depth
Google published results across 19 benchmarks vs Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5.3-Codex
LiveCodeBench Pro Elo 2887 shows strong competitive programming capabilities (~500 ahead of 2nd)
Cost advantage: half the price of Opus 4.6 with comparable reasoning performance

competitors.title: Behind Gemini 3.1 Pro's '13 out of 16 Wins' — The Benchmarks Published and the Benchmarks Left Out
key_points: Google's '13/16 wins' needs context: GPT-5.3-Codex left most benchmarks unpublished
Third-party data tells different story: Arena user voting shows Opus 4.6 just 4 points ahead (essentially tied)
GDPval-AA (enterprise tasks) shows Claude models lead by ~300 points (Sonnet 4.6 scored 1633 vs Gemini 1317)
Cost is clear differentiator: Less than half the cost of Opus 4.6 with comparable reasoning performance
Asymmetry in benchmark disclosure: winning against absent competitors doesn't prove dominance

stats.fact: Gemini 3.1 Pro scored 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2, more than double Gemini 3 Pro's 35-38%
Intelligence Index score of 57 vs peer median of 26 — 2x advantage over average frontier model
29-second time-to-first-token vs peer median of 1.20 seconds (24x slower)
LiveCodeBench Pro Elo 2887 — ~500 ahead of second place for competitive programming
Identical API pricing as Gemini 3.0 Pro ($2.00/1M input, $12.00/1M output)
GDPval-AA enterprise tasks: Gemini 3.1 Pro scored 1317 vs Sonnet 4.6's 1633 (300+ point gap)
Cost advantage: less than half the price of Opus 4.6 with comparable reasoning
BrowseComp 85.9%, MCP Atlas 69.2% for agentic tasks (search & MCP)

angles: Benchmark vs real-world performance: Understanding the asymmetry in benchmark disclosure
Cost-performance ratio: Gemini 3.1 Pro offers comparable reasoning at half the price of Opus 4.6
Latency trade-off: 29-second TTFT is real handicap for interactive vs async applications
Developer perspective: Coding benchmarks (Terminal-Bench, LiveCodeBench) vs general reasoning
Enterprise gap: Strong in reasoning/science but trails in enterprise tasks (GDPval-AA)
Preview vs production: Still in preview, but API pricing advantage immediate
Apple Siri integration: Multi-year deal announced for iOS 26.4 and WWDC 2026
outline: Introduction: Gemini 3.1 Pro launch and immediate #1 ranking on Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index
Core improvement: ARC-AGI-2 77.1% vs Gemini 3 Pro's 35-38% — reasoning breakthrough explained
Performance trade-off: 29-second time-to-first-token and extended thinking architecture
Coding benchmarks: Terminal-Bench 2.0, LiveCodeBench Pro, SWE-Bench Verified results
Competitor comparison: How Gemini 3.1 Pro stacks against Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3-Codex
Cost advantage analysis: Less than half the price of Opus 4.6 with comparable reasoning
GDPval-AA gap: Enterprise tasks (finance, legal) where Claude models still lead
Apple Siri integration: What developers should expect from iOS 26.4 and WWDC 2026
Use case recommendations: When to choose Gemini 3.1 Pro vs alternatives
Agentic workflows: Search+Code, BrowseComp, MCP strengths for multi-step tasks
Developer API access: Google AI Studio, Vertex AI, NotebookLM, Gemini CLI options
Long context advantage: 1M token window for codebase/document analysis
View from third-party platforms: Arena, Artificial Analysis, and benchmark disclosure asymmetry
Production readiness: Preview status, rate limits, and GA timeline considerations
Conclusion: Gemini 3.1 Pro as cost-effective reasoning powerhouse with clear trade-offs
Appendix: Full benchmark table comparisons and API pricing breakdown
